description.value:
Trigger The connection between one pair of psychic anchors is severed

Effect Magitech panels embedded in the floor energize with a high-pitched whine that increases in volume until becoming nearly unbearable. With an ear-splitting screech, energized tendrils manifest from the walls, ceiling, and floor of the complex. All creatures in area A2 and A5 take @Damage[(1d10+4)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save). The hazard rolls initiative.

details.description: The magitech mechanisms that control the works of the complex in the heart of the Wreck attempt to restore the severed connection between the psychic anchors, flooding the area with dangerous energetic tendrils.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] to disrupt the flow of magical energy in the floor panels; @Check[crafting|dc:20] or @Check[thievery|dc:20] to disable the magitech floor panels; @Check[computers|dc:22] to deactivate the subroutine (must be attempted at one of the computers in the psychic anchor rooms). Three total successes required.

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-26: Final Gambit: Part Two

details.reset: The trap deactivates and resets when all psychic anchors are active. If disabled, this hazard instead resets in 24 hours.
details.routine: (2 actions) The hazard makes three probing lash strikes against randomly chosen PCs in area A2 and A5. On the 5th round and every 5 rounds thereafter, the one deactivated psychic anchor becomes active and, if both psychic anchors in that pair are active, the energy beam connecting them is restored. A broken or destroyed psychic anchor can't be activated in this way.
